headline: They Paved the Fishing Ground and Called It Progress
context: Penang South Reclamation will create 1,800 hectares of new land by filling active coastal fishing zones that sustain 4,000 fishing families.

cards.big: 1,800 hectares of new land. 4,000 fishing families displaced.
cards.sub: PSR is Penang's largest infrastructure project. It sits on top of a working fishery.

cards.big: Orang Laut and Malay fishers hold customary rights predating Independence.
cards.sub: No NCR assessment was conducted. Compensation calculated on licence value, not livelihood.

cards.big: EIA approved despite DOE concerns over sediment plume modelling.
cards.sub: The approval letter contained 72 conditions — more than any previous Malaysian EIA.

cards.big: Projected revenue: RM70B over 30 years. Who benefits?
cards.sub: SRS Consortium is 60% state-linked. Land sale profits do not flow to displaced communities.

cards.big: Development is not the question. Distribution is.
cards.sub: When RM70B is generated and fishing families get RM15,000 each, the model is extraction.

cards.big: Coastal communities are not obstacles to progress. They are people with rights that predate the blueprint.
